subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 2 May
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 2 May.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Performance Dashboard (Apr full-month)

A/ Topline/P&L

** Local SIP Apr full month P&L is after pass-back of $200k clawed back
amount from ID fraud sellers, but before tax passback and SLS compensation

* CN SIP Apr full month P&L is 1) preliminary after BI script change, and
2) before CB FBS SIP P&L inclusion


Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Apr ADO 179k, 104% RR; ADO as %CB 113% RR; ADG $2.2M, 111%RR;
   ADG as %CB 116% RR
   -

      Local SIP ADO 97% RR, %CB -0.8pp MoM; RR recovering mainly driven by
      COD reopening of “safe” IDSIP sellers with 85% COD ADO coverage
      -

      CNSIP ADO 111% RR, %CB +0.1pp MoM; driven by SIP optimization
      initiatives 1) price reduction; 2) new SKU ads incubation
      2.

   [P&L] Apr local SIP seller PC2 -$2.0M, $1.8M below target for seller P&L
   due to IDSIP fraud case



Loss to SIP

Remark

(1) Loss on Apr 17th

$1,081,026

-> updated to Chris in CB meeting

(2) New fraud findings (found on Apr 30th)

~$500K

-> By improved fraud check model, and further manual checks

(3) Additional ads/voucher

~$200K

-> Push to maintain ADO runrate at 96% and mitigate the COD closure impact
(aligned with ID side)

Total (1)+(2)+(3)

1.77 million

Vs SIP Seller PL target of April


   3.

    Combined PC2 at shopee level -205k, $2.3M below target due to IDSIP
   fraud case
   -

      $1.8M from seller P&L loss (in #2)
      -

      $560k from MP PC2 gap va target, mainly due to 1) topline missing
      target (on net order basis ADG at 99% run-rate); 2) higher than expected
      platform CIR%


B/ SIP fraud management update


   1.

   Fraud detection
   1.

      IDSIP: Total 303 fraud sellers detected so far; loss to shopee
      $1.6mil; $200k clawed back
      2.

      VNSIP: Total 26 fraud sellers detected so far; loss to shopee $3.5k;
      COD order settlement is extended for 10 days, the 33 sellers’
cod was off

*[P&L] Apr local SIP seller PC2 -$2.0M, $1.8M below target for seller
* Combined PC2 at shopee level -205k, $2.3M below target due to IDSIP

Other SIPs: no obvious fraud detected with security strategy team
Continuous daily scanning by fraud detection model + manual check in
85% COD ADO coverage) reopened
Next step for biz security to suggest additional group of “safe”
Offline investigation on ID fraud sellers to potentially recover
Long-term product solution: CB BPM in discussion with dev team on
MYSG SIP 90% RR, +0.2 day MoM / MYSG LFF 98% RR, +0.2 day MoM: Mainly
VNMY 97% RR, +0.1 day MoM: Logistics vendor missing SLA requirement;

According to MY SPX, LT started to recover since 21 Apr; expect to be
Key reasons for slow recovery back to Feb level, due to HC/hiring
Agent CSAT: overall hitting target across markets, with overall RR
Agent productivity: As of 27 Apr, MY productivity remains low at 5.6
*Agent Avg Response time: Overall missing target with 66% RR.*
Low Chatbot CSAT due to non-LLM chatbot; reviewed A/B testing results

#4 Local FBS x SIP enhancement pending marketplace (order) TD review
#7 FRF reviewed with FPM. Target to UAT on May 16th and Live on 22/May
#9 Aligned with VN local team about the customized features for VNMY
#10 Long-term solution to cover all resync reasons pending FRF review
#12 POP ticket raised, pending RSX approval
#13 POP ticket raised, pending RBX approval
#15 To conduct FRF review and obtain sign-off with business teams ETA
#16 Raised as P0 to ROX, pending approval

Competitiveness vs Temu: assortment, price, logistics service
Revisit direct shipping service options: BWT, CPO, and how to close
clear legal for auto-enrollment cases

content.answer: Apr MY SPX FM LT 1.5 days, 0.4 days longer than 1.1 days for Feb

According to MY SPX, LT started to recover since 21 Apr; expect to be
   1.3 days in May, and back to 1.1 days in Jun

Key reasons for slow recovery back to Feb level, due to HC/hiring
   gap. HC is not filled as planned and unable to hire sufficient part-timers



Do not understand. @'Lee Sun Cheah' <leesun.cheah@spxexpress.com> you know
what is going on here?


[image: image.png]


March and April generally due to the high volume and post raya backlog /
congestion issues


While in May, what we observe is that

(1) Relocation of JB RC end of Feb (no longer have the synergy of being in
the same WH as JB RC, required LH to transports goods to SIP WH

(2) Start of May with just post 5.5 volume (slightly elevated ADT), we
think should normalise to ~1.2


Next steps:

For immediate, to consider adjusting  LH, Ops shift timings and manpower
to improve handover %

To study SIP sorting plan with BRS come online to shorten overall
processing from SOC time in Q3



Best,

Lee Sun

Hi Ian,

Direct selling

How is direct adoption coming along with the new seller centre? In terms of
# of sellers, skus, ado?


   -

   We have whitelisted all existing 6580 MYSG Direct sellers to migrate to
   new GSC as of 23 Apr
   -

      MTD 115 active sellers, 19k SKUs (60% of their MY SKUs, in line with
      SKU migration projection); MTD ADO 6
      -

      Next steps to accelerate the shop opening & active selling with 1)
      “carrots” and “sticks” from MYSG team (0% comm waiver till 30
Jun, SG shop
      platform support to cut by 30 May); 2) product solution to bypass seller
      manual processes and open the shop for the seller [ETA 18 May]
      -

      Target to complete shop opening of all 6580 sellers to start selling
      by end May
      -

   Meanwhile we have kicked off the new seller acquisition from 23 Apr
   -

      MTD 321 sellers accepted and in the onboarding process; MTD active
      sellers 4 (1.3% RR vs May target of 298 new active sellers)
      -

      Next step to 1) push accepted sellers to open shop by end May; 2)
      continue to pitch target pool of 900 sellers for May

According to MY SPX, LT started to recover since 21 Apr; expect to
Key reasons for slow recovery back to Feb level, due to HC/hiring
For immediate, to consider adjusting  LH, Ops shift timings and manpower

content.answer:
Hi Ian,
Sorry coming back on this late.

When we look at ops clock, main gap is due to the delays in receiving of
volume to SIP warehouse that lead to
Cut off time 1 <3pm has reduced ~8% for May to 36.2% vs 44.42% in Feb,
though same day handover % remain high at >98.5%

Ops clock:
[image: image.png]
Same day handover:
[image: image.png]

We will work with @Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com> and @Huang Wan-Jung
<wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com> on how critical is to bring up the CO1 1
handover % (and at what % would really improve SG SPX downstream ADT), and
we layout the plans to achieve that

Best,
Lee Sun

content.question: Hmm ok, so we are not really sure what changed from before Raya?
Can let me know what the plans are after discussing?
Apr MY SPX FM LT 1.5 days, 0.4 days longer than 1.1 days for Feb
According to MY SPX, LT started to recover since 21 Apr; expect to
Key reasons for slow recovery back to Feb level, due to HC/hiring
For immediate, to consider adjusting  LH, Ops shift timings and manpower
To study SIP sorting plan with BRS come online to shorten overall
